title: UC0 — Install kagent
description: Run this first. Install kagent on your slice — every Artemis mission tour assumes a working agent runtime is already on the cluster.

steps.title: Check your cluster
steps.explanation:
Before installing kagent, confirm your slice is reachable.

- `kubectl config current-context` should show your participant slice.
- `kubectl get nodes` should list at least one node in `Ready` state. If nothing comes back, raise it with the workshop crew before continuing.

steps.title: Install kagent via CLI
steps.explanation:
kagent ships a CLI that handles the full install — CRDs, controller, and the demo profile's pre-packaged agents (Prometheus / Grafana agents shipped alongside the controller).

- `--profile demo` is the broadest install — controller plus the pre-packaged Prometheus / Grafana agents that observability missions rely on. The other documented profile, `--profile minimal`, ships the controller alone.
- `--timeout 15m` overrides the CLI default of 5 min — enough headroom for a first-time install to settle without the wait phase failing.
- The CLI installs into the `kagent` namespace by default.
- `kubectl rollout status` then waits for the controller to be `Available` before we move on.

steps.title: List the kagent CRDs
steps.explanation:
kagent extends Kubernetes with its own custom resource kinds (CRDs). The API server now knows how to handle an `Agent` the same way it handles a `Deployment`, which is what lets every Artemis tour describe its agent in YAML and `kubectl apply` it.

Run the command below to confirm the new kinds are registered. You should see at least:

- `agents.kagent.dev`: the agent itself (model, tools, prompt).
- `modelconfigs.kagent.dev`: the LLM binding.
- `toolservers.kagent.dev` and `remotemcpservers.kagent.dev`: the tool surface.

These kinds are what every UC tour will create.

steps.title: Find the controller pod
steps.explanation:
The kagent **controller** is the workhorse that watches your CRDs and turns them into running things. When you create an `Agent` resource, the controller wires up its tools, binds the model, and stands up the request surface so the agent can actually answer questions.

The command below lists every pod in the `kagent` namespace. You want a `kagent-controller-...` pod in `Running` state, alongside a few helpers (UI, tool servers, postgres, etc.).

commands.label: List pods in the kagent namespace

steps.title: See the default ModelConfig
steps.explanation:
A `ModelConfig` is how an agent declares which LLM to use: provider, model name, and a reference to the credentials. Every agent points at a ModelConfig by name.

The demo profile pre-creates `default-model-config` so every Artemis agent (UC1 through UC4) can reuse the same one without each scenario wiring up its own credentials. The command below should show exactly that single ModelConfig in the `kagent` namespace.

steps.title: Take a look at the dashboard
steps.explanation:
kagent is up and the resources you just inspected (CRDs, controller, ModelConfig) are all reachable from a single web UI. Open it now so it is not a surprise when UC1 asks you to chat with an agent in it.

Use the **dashboard** button in this workshop-tour extension (top of the tour pane) to open the kagent web dashboard in your browser. You should see the kagent UI load with the empty agents list (per-UC agents land later, on each scenario's apply).

Close the browser tab when done or leave it open for UC1. UC0 is complete.
